title: phyllachne rubra
intro: Phyllachne rubra, commonly known as Red Flowering Tree Pea, is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand. This plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, which include promoting relaxation and reducing stress due to its therapeutic actions. The health benefits of Phyllachne rubra include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, thanks to its bioactive constituents such as flavonoids and alkaloids. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's effects and potential interactions with other medications.
parts: Phyllachne rubra has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, leaves, and seeds, which are utilized in traditional medicine. The roots of Phyllachne rubra are reported to contain alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The leaves of the plant are said to contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The seeds of Phyllachne rubra are also used medicinally, containing saponins, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are reported to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory activities.
side_effects: Phyllachne rubra can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of anthraquinone glycosides. Ingesting large amounts can also lead to kidney damage and electrolyte imbalances. Skin irritation and allergic reactions have been reported in individuals who handle the plant without proper protection. Additionally, overuse of Phyllachne rubra can exacerbate existing conditions, such as bleeding disorders and ulcers.
